description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a ribbed texture. The grooves are deep and uniform, giving the block a distinctive striped appearance when viewed from above. The sides of the block are smooth and solid, with a dark, almost black coloration, while the top surface has a mottled gray stone texture. The block appears dense and heavy, likely made from a solid stone material such as granite or basalt. Its design suggests it could serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, possibly to control pedestrian or vehicular movement. There are no visible markings, engravings, or signs of significant weathering on the surface.
description: A heavy, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface and smooth, dark sides. The top is mottled gray, giving it a robust and urban appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the parallel grooves running horizontally across the top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the grooves running vertically from this perspective.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the horizontal grooves and the block's solid, rectangular form.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the vertical grooves and the block's thickness.
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no signs of erosion or moss.
